chapter_context.summary: Contrasts the complete, admirable obedience of the Rechabites (who refused to drink wine per their father's command) with Judah's persistent disobedience.

outline.text: Rechabites' Complete Obedience to Jonadab's Commands
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rechabites' Complete Obedience to Jonadab's Commands (35:1-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Contrasting Rechabites' Obedience with Judah's Rebellion
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Contrasting Rechabites' Obedience with Judah's Rebellion (35:12-19), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Conflict and Exile
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of conflict and exile, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Weeping Prophet
key_themes.description: Jeremiah's deep emotional connection to the message and the people, illustrating the heart of God breaking for His children's rebellion.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and historical accounts of Chapter 35, in what ways does Jeremiah's call to faithfulness encourage you in your own walk today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose covenant obedience and trust in God's promises of restoration over temporary security?

cultural_historical.title: The Fall of Jerusalem and the Exile
cultural_historical.text: The historical backdrop of Jeremiah's ministry, witnessing the gradual collapse of Judah under the Babylonian Empire and the destruction of the Temple.
